title: Ianthe occidentalis
intro: Ianthe occidentalis, also known as Western Ianthe, is a perennial plant species in the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its delicate blue flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ianthe occidentalis include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to relieve colds, coughs, and sore throats.
